text: The world around me feels meticulously composed—there is a clear order and intention in these spaces. The glass meeting room, quiet and expectant, stands in contrast with the energetic, idea-saturated display behind the 'R+D' wall. As I hear the oddly jumbled history of the London Bridge, full of contradictions and temporal paradoxes, the absurdity reminds me how time and facts are as easily bent as our perception of them. In this environment that values research and inquiry, even errors seem to have a kind of meaning, pushing me to reflect on the blurred boundaries between reality, memory, and invention. I feel at once small and essential, a quiet observer midstream in humanity's endless conversation.
